headline: D.R. Horton Stock Rises on Earnings Beat. Have Home Builders Hit Bottom?
summary: D.R. Horton  stock rose Tuesday after the home builder reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings.  The company foresees better-than-anticipated home sales gross margins in its third quarter.  Shares were up 6.4% to $163.17 shortly after the market opened on Tuesday after the company reported stronger than expected margins on home sales and estimated that third-quarter margins would be higher than consensus expected.

headline: Alaska Air Suspends Guidance, Citing Jet Fuel Costs. The Stock Is Falling.
summary: Alaska Air Group  reported a wider-than-expected first-quarter loss and suspended full-year guidance as rising fuel costs weigh on the airline.  The air carrier reported a first-quarter loss of $1.68 a share, widening from a loss of 77 cents a year ago.  Alaska Air said Monday that first-quarter fuel costs increased “materially due to elevated crude and refining prices” averaging $2.98 per gallon for the period.

raw.headline: Alaska Air Suspends Guidance, Citing Jet Fuel Costs. The Stock Is Falling.
